journal_entry.description: Revenue recognition for services delivered against customer deposit
journal_entry.rationale: Customer previously paid a $100 deposit, creating a liability (Unearned Revenue or Customer Deposits). Now that the service has been delivered, we decrease the liability (debit) and recognize revenue (credit). This follows IFRS 15 revenue recognition principle: recognize revenue when performance obligation is satisfied. No tax lines are added because the tax would have been collected at the time of the original deposit, not at revenue recognition.

output.reason: When a service is delivered against a customer deposit, the liability (unearned revenue/customer deposit) is decreased (debit side) and service revenue is recognized (credit side). The credit entry is revenue increase = slot c.

credit_classifier.reason: When a service is delivered against a customer deposit, the liability (unearned revenue/customer deposit) is decreased (debit side) and service revenue is recognized (credit side). The credit entry is revenue increase = slot c.

output.reason: Customer deposit is a liability (unearned revenue). When the service is delivered, we recognize the revenue by decreasing the liability = liability decrease (debit to Customer Deposits/Unearned Revenue).

debit_classifier.reason: Customer deposit is a liability (unearned revenue). When the service is delivered, we recognize the revenue by decreasing the liability = liability decrease (debit to Customer Deposits/Unearned Revenue).

output.description: Revenue recognition for services delivered against customer deposit
output.rationale: Customer previously paid a $100 deposit, creating a liability (Unearned Revenue or Customer Deposits). Now that the service has been delivered, we decrease the liability (debit) and recognize revenue (credit). This follows IFRS 15 revenue recognition principle: recognize revenue when performance obligation is satisfied. No tax lines are added because the tax would have been collected at the time of the original deposit, not at revenue recognition.

output.reason: Entry correctly records revenue recognition against customer deposit. Balance verified: $100 debit = $100 credit. Accounts appropriate: Unearned Revenue (liability decrease) debited, Service Revenue (revenue increase) credited. Directionality correct per IFRS 15 - performance obligation satisfied. Rationale correctly notes no additional tax needed as tax was collected with original deposit. All required lines present.
